notes: Multi Studio version 2.1.18: Fixed a playlist display bug in the Scriber where natively dropped or selected audio files showed their unique internal copy name (with microsecond prefixes) instead of the user's original file name., Fixed a drag-and-drop conflict where page dropzones could cross-intercept file drops by scoping native listeners strictly to their active route pathnames., Fixed a bug where generated and imported audio files would sometimes be cleared by the OS or cleaning apps by migrating directories to persistent names (`speech_audio` and `scriber_audio`) and translating path strings on startup., Fixed a bug where the Scriber segment player bar could get stuck at segment 1 due to stale closures in HTML5 Audio event handlers, by introducing ref-backed state handlers to fetch live playback indexes., Fixed a bug where clicking a segment's play icon directly failed to activate its player bar because the auto-follow active segment hook would immediately override the selected index back to the first segment, by restricting auto-selection strictly to active main player sessions., Fixed a layout reflow glitch on active transcript cards by adjusting the segment seeker slider's height to `h-6` to perfectly match the adjacent buttons inside the header container, and restricted active styling strictly to the selected index to prevent multiple cards from highlighting simultaneously., Fixed a bug where playing an individual segment directly left the main combined player's playhead at its old timestamp position, by automatically updating the global jump-to-time sync reference., Fixed a bug where transcript segments failed to scroll into view upon selection when the audio was paused, by binding the auto-scroll observer to trigger on selected segment index changes, and added reference-tracking to guarantee that mouse-leave events do not trigger auto-scroll alignments unless audio is actively playing., Removed hover-dependent hidden opacity configurations from individual segment cards, keeping the playback controls (play, loop, edit) persistently visible., Re-architected segment text editing to occur inplace by replacing paragraph and input tags with a single, unified `<textarea>` component that transitions styling states on focus. The textarea is kept completely borderless and uses a three-tier text color hierarchy (inactive: muted foreground, active/selected: light-gray zinc-200, focused/editing: full high-contrast white), and automatically clears active cursor focus (`blur()`) when Enter (save) or Escape (cancel) is pressed, utilizing a synchronous cancellation reference flag to discard edits on Escape key blur events., Compacted segment card padding and margins, and updated auto-scroll centering behavior to use `block: 'nearest'` to prevent jarring container scroll jumps when clicking segment controllers or playing cards., Added a green, animated "SAVED" feedback badge that appears next to segment timestamps for 1.5 seconds upon successful text updates.
